title: Campaign Analytics
description: Get comprehensive analytics data including transactions, revenue, signups, sessions, and daily performance metrics - matching the Dashboard's Display Reports page.

steps.title: Understanding analytics data sources
steps.description:
CampaignOrder provides analytics data through two relations:

**aggregateData** (JSON-based):
- `completeAttributionSummary`: JSON array with daily metrics
- `byDate`, `topCreatives`, `topPublishers`, `deviceClicks`

**reportDetails** (structured):
- `transaction`: Structured transaction/conversion data with `byDate` breakdowns
- `signups`: Signup metrics with daily breakdowns
- `sessions`: Session metrics with device breakdown

Both relations work through `campaignOrdersConnection` for all permission levels. Not all campaigns have all report types - check for null values.

steps.title: List campaigns with analytics
steps.description: Fetch analytics data for multiple campaigns with filtering. This is the recommended starting point - works with all permission levels.
steps.tip: This query works with all permission levels. Filter by status_in: LIVE for active campaigns, PAST for finished campaigns.

steps.title: Query individual transactions
steps.description: Fetch individual transaction events. Each record represents a conversion attributed to a campaign impression.
steps.tip: Each transaction shows the order ID, revenue, and attribution details (method, window, impressions).

steps.title: Query individual sessions
steps.description: Fetch individual session events. Each record represents a website visit attributed to a campaign impression.
steps.tip: Sessions show visit duration (durationInSeconds), page views, device type, and attribution details.

steps.title: Filter by advertiser
steps.description: Fetch analytics data for all campaigns belonging to a specific advertiser. Useful for aggregating metrics across all campaigns for an organization.
steps.tip: The advertiser field filters campaigns by the organization that owns them. Replace 'your-advertiser-org-id' with the actual org ID.

steps.title: Filter by date range
steps.description: Filter campaigns by their start and end dates. The Dashboard defaults to a 6-month date range.
steps.tip: Use startDate_gte and endDate_lte for date range filtering. The Dashboard defaults to 6 months of data.

steps.title: Understanding completeAttributionSummary
steps.description:
The `completeAttributionSummary` JSON contains daily records with these fields:

- **date**: Date of the metrics
- **impressions**: Total impressions served
- **clicks**: Total clicks
- **ctr**: Click-through rate
- **transactions**: Number of transactions
- **revenue**: Revenue amount
- **spend**: Amount spent
- **sessions**: Number of sessions
- **signUps**: Number of sign-ups

This matches the columns shown in the Dashboard's Display Reports table.
steps.tip: Parse the JSON response to extract daily metrics for reporting.

steps.title: Query reportDetails via connection
steps.description: Fetch structured analytics including transactions, signups, and sessions using the connection query. This works with all permission levels.
steps.tip: Not all campaigns have all report types - transaction/signups/sessions may be null if not configured for the campaign.

tips: All queries use campaignOrdersConnection - works with all permission levels
Filter by advertiser.id to get campaigns for a specific organization
Not all campaigns have signups/sessions/transactions - check for null values
aggregateData has JSON fields, reportDetails has structured typed data
Common status values: LIVE, PAST, APPROVED, PAUSED, DRAFT
Use orderBy: startDate_DESC to show most recent campaigns first
Combine with pacingData for a complete picture of campaign performance
